title: cuphea calophylla var. orthodisca
intro: Cuphea calophylla var. orthodisca, also known as Firecracker Plant, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its bright red or yellow flowers that resemble tiny fireworks. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cuphea calophylla var. orthodisca include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and support cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
